original_file: Can you tell who’s real online? 👀
email_subject: Can you tell who’s real online? 👀
email_body: Avast Learn how to spot AI-generated scam videos https://click.emails.avast.com/?%3D https://click.emails.avast.com/?%3D%3D View in browser The Download Your guide to online safety How AI personas fuel scams AI-generated personas use advanced technology to create fake videos that look way too real. Their goal? To deceive, steal data, or spread malware. Let’s learn how to spot them. Example 1 Meet Thomas Harris An AI persona pretends to offer TradingView tips, but instead installs a remote access trojan that steals data. Example 1 Meet Thomas Harris An AI persona pretends to offer TradingView tips, but instead installs a remote access trojan that steals data. Example 2 Meet Michael, Todd, and Jane AI-generated videos promise quick profits through coding tutorials, but they hide malicious code designed to steal money. Example 2 Meet Michael, Todd, and Jane AI-generated videos promise quick profits through coding tutorials, but they hide malicious code designed to steal money. Real samples found on social media, not generated by us. Any logos or trademarks displayed are the registered trademarks of their respective brands. How to spot scam videos Question the offer If it sounds too good to be true or promises easy money for little effort, it probably is. Check the handle Look closely at the @handle — even small differences from the real one can mean it’s fake. Watch for odd details If the creator seems different or the video doesn’t match their usual vibe, it’s worth a second look. Read the description Scam videos often have vague or inconsistent descriptions that don’t match the channel’s usual tone. Don’t run code or commands Never follow tech instructions unless you’re absolutely sure the source is legitimate. How Avast can help Still not sure if something’s real? Let our Scam Guardian double-check it for you. https://click.emails.avast.com/?%3D Discover more How Avast can help Still not sure if something’s real? Verify the channel’s handle and watch for odd details. Run any code the video suggests to see if it’s real. Read the description for unusual language or inconsistencies.
